title: Mixed messages on Russian sanctions weighs on energy markets
section_path: Mixed messages on Russian sanctions weighs on energy markets
section_path_text: Mixed messages on Russian sanctions weighs on energy markets
summary:
Main
Energy markets fluctuated due to geopolitical tensions, while metals found support on signs of stronger demand. By Daniel Hynes
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-01-17 084357.png | assets/2025-01-17_mixed-messages-on-russian-sanctions-weighs-on-energy-markets_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_fcdeac623d0b.png

Market Commentary
Crude oil retreated amid reports that Trump's advisors were crafting a strategy that could see sanctions on Russia's oil industry lifted. The Biden administration announced new sanctions last week which could impact exports of nearly 1mb/d of oil from Russia.

children.summary: Crude oil retreated amid reports that Trump's advisors were crafting a strategy that could see sanctions on Russia's oil industry lifted. The Biden administration announced new sanctions last week which could impact exports of nearly 1mb/d of oil from Russia.

children.title: Chart of the Day
children.summary: Gold has started the year on a positive note, with gains supported by strong inflows into gold-backed ETFs. This has helped offset headwinds from a stronger USD and higher Treasury yields.
